name: createRequestComment
description:
The request ID must contain the team ID as a prefix, in \`teamId-requestId\` format.

For example, if you're creating a comment on collection ID \`24585957-7b2c98f7-30db-4b67-8685-0079f48a0947\` (note on the prefix), and
the collection request's ID is \`2c450b59-9bbf-729b-6ac0-f92535a7c336\`, then the \`{requestId}\` must be \`24585957-2c450b59-9bbf-729b-6ac0-f92535a7c336\`.

requestId.description:
The request ID must contain the team ID as a prefix, in `teamId-requestId` format.

For example, if you're creating a comment on collection ID `24585957-7b2c98f7-30db-4b67-8685-0079f48a0947` (note on the prefix), and
the collection request's ID is `2c450b59-9bbf-729b-6ac0-f92535a7c336`, then the `{requestId}` must be `24585957-2c450b59-9bbf-729b-6ac0-f92535a7c336`.

threadId.description: The comment's thread ID. To create a reply on an existing comment, include this property.

{{userName}}.description: An object that contains information about the tagged user. The object's name is the user's Postman username. For example, `@user-postman`.

tags.description: Information about users tagged in the `body` comment.
